2605.26691.title: Mind the Tool Failures: Achieving Synergistic Tool Gains for Medical Agents
2605.26691.paper_summary: Medical AI agents increasingly use external tools for diagnosis, treatment recommendation, and evidence retrieval, yet most existing approaches assume that task-appropriate tools are reliable within their intended scope. This assumption is fragile in real clinical settings, where even relevant tools may fail on challenging instances and lead to unsafe downstream decisions. To address this issue, we study medical tool use under imperfect-tool settings to correct failure instances missed by individual tools. Instance-dependent failure patterns create a gap between the best fixed single tool and an ideal instance-wise selector, which we refer to as the Single-Oracle risk gap. The core challenge is that conventional task-level tool selection cannot realize this gap, as it is inherently bounded by the performance of the best single tool. Motivated by this observation, we therefore account for instance-level heterogeneity and formulate tool use as an instance-level selection problem. Particularly, we propose a GRPO-based reinforcement learning framework with rewards for probabilistic risk minimization and disagreement-aware synergy learning, which promotes instance-level correction of erroneous tool consensus. Furthermore, an entropy-guided sampling strategy is adopted to upweight high-disagreement instances, which provide stronger signals for learning instance-specific tool synergy. These two components complement each other in mitigating instance-level heterogeneity and improving tool synergy. Experiments on two tasks and seven medical benchmarks show that our method consistently achieves robust and stable improvements over a broad range of baselines, highlighting the importance of synergy-aware tool use for reliable medical agentic systems.
2605.26691.paper_summary_zh: 醫療 AI 代理人越來越多地使用外部工具進行診斷、治療建議和證據檢索，但大多數現有方法假設任務適當的工具在其預期範圍內是可靠的。這一假設在實際臨床環境中是脆弱的，即使是相關工具在面對挑戰性實例時也可能失敗，並導致不安全的下游決策。為了解決這一問題，我們研究在不完美工具設置下的醫療工具使用，以修正個別工具未能識別的失敗實例。實例依賴的失敗模式在最佳固定單一工具與理想的實例級選擇器之間創造了一個差距，我們稱之為單一預言者風險差距。核心挑戰在於，傳統的任務級工具選擇無法實現這一差距，因為它本質上受到最佳單一工具性能的限制。基於這一觀察，我們因此考慮實例級的異質性，並將工具使用形式化為一個實例級選擇問題。特別地，我們提出了一個基於 GRPO 的強化學習框架，該框架對於概率風險最小化和考慮分歧的協同學習給予獎勵，促進對錯誤工具共識的實例級修正。此外，採用熵引導的抽樣策略來加權高分歧實例，這些實例為學習實例特定的工具協同提供了更強的信號。這兩個組件相輔相成，以減輕實例級異質性並改善工具協同。在兩個任務和七個醫療基準上的實驗顯示，我們的方法在廣泛的基準上始終實現穩健和穩定的改進，突顯了考慮協同的工具使用對於可靠醫療代理系統的重要性。
2605.26691.author: Yunhui Gan et.al.
2605.26691.authors: Yunhui Gan, Tan Pan, Kaiyu Guo, Limei Han, Weimiao Yu, Guangnan Ye, Chen Jiang, Yuan Cheng
